title: pelargonium × rosenthalianum
intro: Pelargonium × rosenthalianum, also known as Rosalina, is a hybrid species of the pelargonium plant, which belongs to the geranium family. It is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Rosalina include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of geraniol and linalool. The herbal preparations of Rosalina, such as tea and essential oil, have been used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, making it a popular remedy in natural health practices.
